title: Analysis of whole blood using flow cytometry
abstract: The present disclosure relates to systems and methods for analyzing particles within a flow cytometry system. A method for analyzing particles within a whole blood sample includes receiving a whole blood sample. Furthermore, the method includes receiving a panel selection relating to one or more constituents within the whole blood sample, wherein the one or more constituents include one or more desired constituents. Furthermore, the method includes adding at least one labeling reagent to the whole blood sample, wherein the at least one labeling reagent is configured to label the one or more desired constituents. Further yet, the method includes collecting flow cytometry data from the whole blood sample and analyzing the one or more constituents.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for analyzing particles within a flow cytometry system, the method comprising: receiving a whole blood sample; receiving a panel selection corresponding to one or more constituents within the whole blood sample, wherein the one or more constituents include one or more desired constituents; adding at least one labeling reagent to the whole blood sample, the at least one labeling reagent being configured to label the one or more desired constituents; collecting flow cytometry data from the whole blood sample; and analyzing the one or more constituents by: generating one or more waveforms, the one or more waveforms including events that correspond to the one or more desired constituents within the whole blood sample; and analyzing the one or more waveforms to identify the events that correspond to white blood cells. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising transmitting the flow cytometry' data to an electronic system, wherein the electronic system includes a waveform acquisition device and a waveform analysis device. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more waveforms are generated using the waveform acquisition device and the one or more constituents are analyzed using the waveform analysis device. 4. The method of any' one of claims 1-3, wherein the at least one labeling reagent is added to the whole blood sample without adding a lytic agent, diluting the whole blood sample, or washing the whole blood sample. 5. The method of any' one of claims 1-4, wherein the panel selection includes fluorochrome-labeled antibodies. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the panel selection includes fluorescein isothiocyanate. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the fluorescein isothiocyanate is used to label at least one monoclonal antibody, wherein the at least one monoclonal antibody includes at least one CD45 monoclonal antibody. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the flow cytometry system includes: a light source for generating a light beam toward an interrogation zone; and an optical system including detectors for detecting radiated light from the one or more constituents passing through the light beam in the interrogation zone. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the flow cytometry data is generated by using the light source and the optical system to detect the one or more constituents that pass through the interrogation zone. 10. A method for analyzing flow cytometry data, the method comprising: receiving flow cytometry data, wherein the flow cytometry data includes one or more waveforms that are representative of characteristics of whole blood constituents within a whole blood sample, the whole blood constituents include at least one constituent of interest and at least one other constituent; identifying the at least one constituent of interest; isolating at least one event pertaining to the at least one constituent of interest by identifying at least one event that includes the characteristics that indicate the at least one constituent of interest was detected; and analyzing the at least one constituent of interest independently from the at least one other constituent. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the one or more waveforms include one or more of side scatter data, forward scatter data, and fluorescent channel data, or a time duration for the whole blood constituents to pass through a light source within a flow cytometry system. 12. The method of claim 11. wherein the fluorescent channel data includes fluorochrome-labeled antibodies. 13. The method of claim 12. wherein the fluorochrome-labeled antibodies include a CD45 fluorochrome-labeled antibody. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the at least one constituent of interest is identified by isolating the at least one event within the flow cytometry data that includes peak intensities in the side scatter data, the forward scatter data, and the fluorescent channel data, wherein the fluorescent channel data includes the CD45 fluorochrome-labeled antibody. 15. The method of any one of claims 10-14. wherein the at least one constituent of interest includes one or more white blood cells.
